整套首页
unit_17 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_17

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 287 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)______ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)______ about the new location: the building’s design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)______ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)______ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)______ about the new location: the building’s design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)______ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　an unprepossessing; spare; a grandiose
bureaucraticbaroque
bureaucratic: standard unit headword | baroque: standard unit headword
#2 Text Completion
Britain’s deteriorating economy after 1945 was (i)______ by politicians who favored the manufacturing sector over the service sector: rather than attempting to (ii)______ the decline of manufacturing, they should have promoted service industries.Britain’s deteriorating economy after 1945 was (i)______ by politicians who favored the manufacturing sector over the service sector: rather than attempting to (ii)______ the decline of manufacturing, they should have promoted service industries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　mishandled; arrest
favorarrestbolsterdeteriorate
favor: standard unit headword | arrest: standard unit headword | bolster: standard unit headword | deteriorate: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) ____________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) ____________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded
hypothesisdisrupt
hypothesis: standard unit headword | disrupt: standard unit headword
#4 SE
Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.
答案：C, E　a derisive; a contemptuous
ingenuousbenign
ingenuous: standard unit headword | benign: standard unit headword
#5 TC TRIPLE
Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored
punctilioussurvey
punctilious: standard unit headword | survey: standard unit headword
#6 Sentence Equivalence
Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by ______ some kinds of knowledge as real science.Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by ______ some kinds of knowledge as real science.
答案：B, F　sanctioning; endorsing
differentiatecensuresanction
differentiate: standard unit headword | censure: standard unit headword | sanction: standard unit headword
#7 TC SINGLE
The myth of the small town as a repository of innocence and contentment has frequently been __________ in literature and film by depictions of rural life as plagued by worldly ambitions.The myth of the small town as a repository of innocence and contentment has frequently been __________ in literature and film by depictions of rural life as plagued by worldly ambitions.
答案：B　interrogated
repository
repository: standard unit headword
#8 TC DOUBLE
When an organism is functioning normally, excessive fat is stored in adipose tissue. Thus, the (i) ____________ of such tissue may be (ii) ____________: the fat is diverted to other organs, such as the liver and the heart, causing serious health problems.When an organism is functioning normally, excessive fat is stored in adipose tissue. Thus, the (i) ____________ of such tissue may be (ii) ____________: the fat is diverted to other organs, such as the liver and the heart, causing serious health problems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　dearth; hazardous
divert
divert: standard unit headword

For all the ______ the new CEO has received from the press recently, her staff have a decidedly less rosy view of her.For all the ______ the new CEO has received from the press recently, her staff have a decidedly less rosy view of her.
答案：A, B　encomiums; tributes
encomiumrosy
encomium: standard unit headword | rosy: standard unit headword

Early-twentieth-century India's publishing industry was remarkably __________; publications were offered in languages ranging from regional vernaculars to English and tailored to audiences ranging from working class to elite.Early-twentieth-century India's publishing industry was remarkably __________; publications were offered in languages ranging from regional vernaculars to English and tailored to audiences ranging from working class to elite.
答案：C　segmented
vernacular
vernacular: standard unit headword
#11 TC DOUBLE
Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i)_________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii)_________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i)_________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii)_________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　didactic; amount to
pervade
pervade: standard unit headword
#12 Text Completion
The assumption that children learn about science primarily in the classroom is so ______ that few policy makers question it, despite an ever-growing body of evidence demonstrating that most science is learned outside of school.The assumption that children learn about science primarily in the classroom is so ______ that few policy makers question it, despite an ever-growing body of evidence demonstrating that most science is learned outside of school.
答案：D　pervasive
tenuouspervasive
tenuous: standard unit headword | pervasive: standard unit headword
#13 SE
As if addressing an academic seminar rather than a judicial hearing, the newspaper executive turned __________, pondering not just her own career but also the future of the press in the digital era.As if addressing an academic seminar rather than a judicial hearing, the newspaper executive turned __________, pondering not just her own career but also the future of the press in the digital era.
答案：B, E　contemplative; ruminative
address
address: standard unit headword
#14 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
favorprowess
favor: standard unit headword | prowess: standard unit headword

Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)______ those (ii)______ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)______ research.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　restrain; incursions of; corrupt
unbridledincursion
unbridled: standard unit headword | incursion: standard unit headword
#16 Sentence Equivalence
Historically, the depletion of soil’s nitrogen has been one of the most ______ problems faced by farmers: an essential nutrient, nitrogen is quickly leached from soil, and farmers have struggled to find ways to replenish it.Historically, the depletion of soil’s nitrogen has been one of the most ______ problems faced by farmers: an essential nutrient, nitrogen is quickly leached from soil, and farmers have struggled to find ways to replenish it.
答案：D, E　stubborn; intractable
replenish
replenish: standard unit headword

Thus, it is (ii) that Kaus, during her tenure as county prosecutor, reached all the way to the nation's capital in order to overhaul the office.
Blank 1
Blank 2

Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.
答案：E　a vacuous pastime
incorporate
incorporate: standard unit headword
#19 SE
While the United States Supreme Court has experienced changes over the years, albeit grudgingly at times, one aspect of the Court has been treated as _________ in recent history: the notion that the Court must have only nine members.While the United States Supreme Court has experienced changes over the years, albeit grudgingly at times, one aspect of the Court has been treated as _________ in recent history: the notion that the Court must have only nine members.
答案：C, E　sacrosanct; inviolable
inviolable

答案：A, F　inflammatory; provocative
bighearted
bighearted: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C　disingenuous

答案：D, F　rudimentary; sketchy

Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.

But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood
commentary

Blank 2
Blank 3

O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview

The idea that gold could hasten chemical reactions was (ii)________: after all, gold is (iii)________ in its bulk form. It has been used for millennia to make jewelry precisely because it does not tarnish by reacting with oxygen or water.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　a catalyst; counterintuitive; inert

Blank 2

But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay
immortality
immortality: standard unit headword

答案：B　effecting

irritate: standard unit headword
#52 Sentence Equivalence
Excessive focus on what might have been can cause in us feelings of restlessness and regret, but some scientists are beginning to think that fancying an alternative reality might have ______ effects as well.Excessive focus on what might have been can cause in us feelings of restlessness and regret, but some scientists are beginning to think that fancying an alternative reality might have ______ effects as well.
答案：C, F　restorative; tonic
restorative
restorative: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：C, E　exploited; downplay the legitimacy

答案：C, F　prudent; circumspect

There has been (i)______ elephant’s fabled mental capacities until recently, when these behavioral observations have begun to be (ii)______ by brain science. MRI scans of an elephant’s brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.There has been (i)______ elephant’s fabled mental capacities until recently, when these behavioral observations have begun to be (ii)______ by brain science. MRI scans of an elephant’s brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　only anecdotal evidence for; buttressed

fabled: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　flummoxed; confounded

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　efficiently; vacant

答案：A, B　implacable; unyielding

答案：C, D　sympathetic; well-disposed

答案：B　severe

答案：A, B　tempered; moderated

答案：A, C　porous; permeable

A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) ____________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) ____________ in the face of evidence.A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) ____________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) ____________ in the face of evidence.

Blank 2
答案：C, E　distinct from; intransigent

However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. When native species do decline after nonnative species are introduced, the reason may be a human-caused habitat alteration that affects native and nonnative species (iii) __________.When the introduction of a nonnative species into a habitat correlates with the decline of a species native to that habitat, observers may (i) __________ a causal relationship. Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：C, E, H　infer; unusual; differentially

On the contrary, it is difficult to think of a writer of equal fame and importance who, on close inspection, proves to be such a (iii) __________ human being.Although Dostoyevsky was not the (i) __________ portrayed by some biographers, at least implicitly guilty of the terrible crimes he depicted in his novels, he was notoriously (ii) __________. 答案：B, D　Bolstering; Buttressing

Given So’s (iii)______ many of the players in Hong Kong politics, it is surprising that he did not use interviews and other forms of direct research to delve further into the motivations, strategies, and tactics of participants.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　tempered by; playing a large role in; proximity to

proximity: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　exalt; valorize

encomium: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　reconciliation; rapprochement

Here the review’s task is more (ii)______: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)______ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)______: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the useless and tedious.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from

答案：D　least potent

答案：A, D　slighted; disparaged

答案：C, E　iconoclastic; unorthodox

答案：C, F　specious; unfounded

答案：C, E　Projecting; Extrapolating

答案：C, E　obviate; displace

答案：C, D　assumed; counted on

#179 Text Completion
Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani’s book managed to (i)______ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)______ some of the (iii)______ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani’s book managed to (i)______ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)______ some of the (iii)______ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　confound; puncture; facile
facile

答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable

答案：A, F　unassuming; modest

ingenuous: standard unit headword

But as temperatures rise the permafrost recedes, (i)______ the (ii)______ of forest.Boreal forest is at the southern boundary of the moss-dominated tundra, which remains characteristically treeless because its spongy surface retains water that cannot drain away through the underlying permafrost. But as temperatures rise the permafrost recedes, (i)______ the (ii)______ of forest.

答案：B, D　expel; oust

Blank 2

Blank 2

Blank 2

答案：C, F　burgeoning; expansion

答案：A, E　truculent; pugnacious

brazen: standard unit headword
#192 Text Completion
Many popular musicians have (i)______ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)______ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)______ these developments.Many popular musicians have (i)______ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)______ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)______ these developments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at

Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can ______ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can ______ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.
答案：A, F　hamper; impede

Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very ______ of what science is supposed to be.Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very ______ of what science is supposed to be.
答案：E　antithesis

答案：B, C　circuitous; indirect

Frequently that time happened in a great classroom somewhere, in the hands of the one (ii)______ teacher we can each remember.Many of us remember a time when discovery and the thrill of learning were forces in our lives and were (i)______ . Frequently that time happened in a great classroom somewhere, in the hands of the one (ii)______ teacher we can each remember.

Blank 2

In a competitive news market, producers can use slant to differentiate their products and stave off price competition.

Blank 2

#200 Sentence Equivalence
The town’s air was consistently ______: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal tar factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.The town’s air was consistently ______: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal tar factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.

答案：C, E　tortuous; convoluted

This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.A transformative scientific idea that emerged in the eighteenth century was the realization that slow, inexorable geological processes follow the basic laws of physics and chemistry. This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.

Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　an obvious; followed; revolutionary for

It should be that scientific literacy (iii)______ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)______ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)______. (iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)______ but not always lively and readable. Because leadership in the computer hacker group is extremely (i)______ , when law enforcement agencies arrested several prominent members, taking them offline, the group (ii)______ . Instead, it continues to operate, sometimes under its own name, sometimes under other names. The names are (iii)______ , as are the members of the group: anyone, anywhere, is free to work behind its mask.Because leadership in the computer hacker group is extremely (i)______ , when law enforcement agencies arrested several prominent members, taking them offline, the group (ii)______ . Instead, it continues to operate, sometimes under its own name, sometimes under other names. The names are (iii)______ , as are the members of the group: anyone, anywhere, is free to work behind its mask.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　fluid; did not collapse; fungible
arrest
arrest: standard unit headword

Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)______ her attempt to (iii)______ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)______. 答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain

答案：B, D, I　outstanding; a discernment about; promise

#266 Text Completion
It is a paradox of the Victorians that they are both ______ and, throughout the empire, cosmopolitan.It is a paradox of the Victorians that they are both ______ and, throughout the empire, cosmopolitan.
答案：B　insular
intransigent
intransigent: standard unit headword
#267 Text Completion
For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound
disingenuous
disingenuous: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　tendentious; selective; omitting

#270 Text Completion
Elephants are known to use a wide variety of calls, many of them infrasonic (below the level of human hearing), in coordinating group behavior. The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)______ , but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)______ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)______ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.Elephants are known to use a wide variety of calls, many of them infrasonic (below the level of human hearing), in coordinating group behavior. The infrasonic calls usually have a component that is (i)______ , but these higher frequency aspects of the calls (ii)______ very quickly with distance, so that in the wild, human observers are often (iii)______ sudden coordinated movements of large groups of elephants when no signal was apparent to the observer.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　humanly audible; attenuate; astonished by

differentiate: standard unit headword
#271 Text Completion
The quiet, careful member of a collaborative, creative pair may naturally be (i)______ by posterity in comparison with the brash, dramatic one. Braque and Picasso created Cubism together, but the mercurial Picasso is regarded as the star of the movement while Braque, because he was so much more (ii)______ , has been relatively (iii)______ by history.The quiet, careful member of a collaborative, creative pair may naturally be (i)______ by posterity in comparison with the brash, dramatic one. Braque and Picasso created Cubism together, but the mercurial Picasso is regarded as the star of the movement while Braque, because he was so much more (ii)______ , has been relatively (iii)______ by history.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　diminished; understated; neglected

favor: standard unit headword
#272 Text Completion
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; apt; impugned

conflate: standard unit headword

答案：D　outstrips

The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity
